title: Storyboard sync reliability — git push fix, LaunchAgent proposed
summary: Diagnosed why sessions weren't appearing on GitHub Pages: file was written but not pushed. Guided git push to fix. Discussed architectural limit: sync task only runs when Cowork is open, can't guarantee no missed runs. Proposed LaunchAgent as OS-level fix — a shell script running every 15 min to git push after Cowork sync writes the file, independent of app state.
decisions: git push was the immediate fix — commits existed, just not pushed
LaunchAgent proposed as permanent fix for push reliability
Scheduler architectural limit: sync only runs with Cowork open
ideas: Build Mac LaunchAgent that runs git push every 15 min — OS-level reliability
LaunchAgent would work even when Cowork is closed or Mac restarts

title: Session sync: git push blocked by concurrent Storyboard brain process
summary: The scheduled storyboard session sync ran and successfully synced 1 new session to session-log.json. The git push was blocked because the actively-running 'Storyboard brain' session held a git index.lock on the same repo. Local file update succeeded; push was deferred until the lock was released.
decisions: Local file update proceeds even if git push fails
Git lock conflicts from concurrent sessions are logged but do not abort the sync
ideas: Consider adding a retry loop for git push to handle transient lock conflicts

title: Session sync: 1 new session logged, git push blocked by index.lock
